decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The most effective primary lever is to increase the heat spreader conductivity to improve lateral heat distribution away from the chip hot spot. A conservative 40% increase is proposed, staying well within the allowed 2x step ratio and material bounds.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity enhances lateral heat spreading, reducing the localized temperature gradient under the chip.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards or below 85.0°C
More uniform temperature profile across the spreader layer
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate conductivity or boundary conditions.
High conductivity values approach the upper bound of typical engineering materials, though still within the defined optimization range.
